data.narrative:
Proteus Space is a Series A–stage space and aerospace company founded by David Kervin and Andrew Shapiro. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 public-market operators (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with primary reliance on 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchored to $17M in total raised applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage transitions, implying a valuation of $34M. The peer-median method across four comparable companies—ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), Hubble Network ($400M, Series B), Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B), and Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B, Series B)—produces a median valuation of $850M. Public-comp anchoring via sector P/S multiples (42.9x median across 6 tickers) is not applicable due to lack of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulated range spans $29M (floor from stage-progression) to $977M (ceiling derived from peer-max scenarios), with a median indication of $442M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Proteus Space's current operational progress relative to comparables and the heterogeneity of commercial space valuations at Series A. Resolution of valuation depends on clarifying revenue traction, technical de-risking, and where the company sits within the peer distribution.
